member.name: T:Pty.Net.PtyDataEventArgs
member.summary: Event args for pty data events.
member.param: The object that fired the event.

member.name: T:Pty.Net.PtyDisconnectedEventArgs
member.summary: Event args for pty disconnection.
member.param: The object that fired the event.

member.name: T:Pty.Net.IPtyConnection
member.summary: This interface represents a connection to a pseudo terminal (PTY).

member.name: E:Pty.Net.IPtyConnection.PtyData
member.summary: Occurs when the Pty has new data that should be processed by the frontend.

member.name: E:Pty.Net.IPtyConnection.PtyDisconnected
member.summary: Occurs when the process connected to the pty has exited and the pty has disconnected.

member.name: M:Pty.Net.IPtyConnection.Write(System.String)
member.summary: Write data to the pty.

member.name: M:Pty.Net.IPtyConnection.Write(System.Char)
member.summary: Write an individual character to the pty.

member.name: M:Pty.Net.IPtyConnection.WriteAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Asynchronousy write data to the pty.
member.returns: A task representing the completion of writing the string.

member.name: M:Pty.Net.IPtyConnection.WriteAsync(System.Char)
member.summary: Asynchronousy write a character to the pty.
member.returns: A task representing the completion of writing the character.

member.name: M:Pty.Net.IPtyConnection.Resize(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Resize the pty.
member.param: The width of the pty in columns.
The height of the pty in rows.

member.name: F:Pty.Net.Native.WinPtyApi.WINPTY_FLAG_CONERR
member.summary: Create a new screen buffer(connected to the "conerr" terminal pipe) and pass it to child processes as the STDERR handle.This flag also prevents the agent from reopening CONOUT$ when it polls -- regardless of whether the active screen buffer changes, winpty continues to monitor the original primary screen buffer.

member.name: F:Pty.Net.Native.WinPtyApi.WINPTY_FLAG_PLAIN_OUTPUT
member.summary: Don't output escape sequences.

member.name: F:Pty.Net.Native.WinPtyApi.WINPTY_FLAG_COLOR_ESCAPES
member.summary: Do output color escape sequences. These escapes are output by default, but are suppressed with WINPTY_FLAG_PLAIN_OUTPUT. Use this flag to reenable them.

member.name: F:Pty.Net.Native.WinPtyApi.WINPTY_FLAG_ALLOW_CURPROC_DESKTOP_CREATION
member.summary: On XP and Vista, winpty needs to put the hidden console on a desktop in a service window station so that its polling does not interfere with other (visible) console windows. To create this desktop, it must change the process' window station (i.e. SetProcessWindowStation) for the duration of the winpty_open call. In theory, this change could interfere with the winpty client (e.g. other threads, spawning children), so winpty by default spawns a special agent process to create the hidden desktop. Spawning processes on Windows is slow, though, so if WINPTY_FLAG_ALLOW_CURPROC_DESKTOP_CREATION is set, winpty changes this process' window station instead. See https://github.com/rprichard/winpty/issues/58.

member.name: F:Pty.Net.Native.WinPtyApi.WINPTY_MOUSE_MODE_NONE
member.summary: QuickEdit mode is initially disabled, and the agent does not send mouse mode sequences to the terminal. If it receives mouse input, though, it still writes MOUSE_EVENT_RECORD values into CONIN.

member.name: F:Pty.Net.Native.WinPtyApi.WINPTY_MOUSE_MODE_AUTO
member.summary:
QuickEdit mode is initially enabled. As CONIN enters or leaves mouse input mode (i.e. where ENABLE_MOUSE_INPUT is on and ENABLE_QUICK_EDIT_MODE is off), the agent enables or disables mouse input on the terminal.
This is the default mode.

member.name: F:Pty.Net.Native.WinPtyApi.WINPTY_MOUSE_MODE_FORCE
member.summary: QuickEdit mode is initially disabled, and the agent enables the terminal's mouse input mode. It does not disable terminal mouse mode (until exit).

member.name: F:Pty.Net.Native.WinPtyApi.WINPTY_SPAWN_FLAG_AUTO_SHUTDOWN
member.summary: If the spawn is marked "auto-shutdown", then the agent shuts down console output once the process exits. The agent stops polling for new console output, and once all pending data has been written to the output pipe, the agent closes the pipe. (At that point, the pipe may still have data in it, which the client may read. Once all the data has been read, further reads return EOF.)

member.name: F:Pty.Net.Native.WinPtyApi.WINPTY_SPAWN_FLAG_EXIT_AFTER_SHUTDOWN
member.summary:
After the agent shuts down output, and after all output has been written into the pipe(s), exit the agent by closing the console. If there any surviving processes still attached to the console, they are killed.
Note: With this flag, an RPC call (e.g. winpty_set_size) issued after the agent exits will fail with an I/O or dead-agent error.

member.name: F:Pty.Net.Native.WinPtyApi.WINPTY_SPAWN_FLAG_MASK
member.summary: All the spawn flags.

member.name: T:Pty.Net.BackendOptions
member.summary: Selects the preferred backend to use.

member.name: F:Pty.Net.BackendOptions.Default
member.summary: Choose the best backend for the current system.

member.name: F:Pty.Net.BackendOptions.WinPty
member.summary: Force WinPty backend.

member.name: F:Pty.Net.BackendOptions.ConPty
member.summary: Force ConPty, this will fail if the API is not available on the current platform.

member.name: T:Pty.Net.PtyProvider
member.summary: Provider for new PTY instances. By default this class will choose the best available PTY backend for the current system. If you are running Windows 10 1809 or newer this backend will use the ConPTY API provided by the platform. On builds earlier than 1809 WinPTY will be used instead.

member.name: M:Pty.Net.PtyProvider.Spawn(System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String,Pty.Net.BackendOptions)
member.summary: Spawn a new instance of
member.param: The executable to run in the PTY.
The width (in columns) of the PTY.
The height (in rows) of the PTY.
The working directory that the PTY will run in. Defaults to the current process directory.
The preferred backend to use, default selects the best for the current platform.
member.returns: An connected to the given process
